prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like rewiring a lit house while you’re still living in it—every new word is a fresh cable snaking through old plaster, sparking when it brushes memories already glowing in the walls. You grope for the breaker box of childhood plasticity, fingers hesitating, half expecting the dark. Yet when the unfamiliar bulb finally flares, it throws colors you swear were never in the rug, the cat, your own face—proof that the mind can still add wings to the light it lives by.
